title: Emergency Access Procedure — Downtime / Break-Glass Account Usage

compliance.assurance_rationale: HIPAA Security §164.312(a)(2)(ii) — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that HIPAA Security §164.312(a)(2)(ii) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.10.14: Emergency Access Procedure — Downtime / Break-Glass Account Usage.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.10.14' running on WinEventLog:Security 4624 for svc_epic_emergency*, index=epic sourcetype=epic:audit AccessContext="Downtime", archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
spl:
(index=windows EventCode="4622" OR EventCode="4624") Account_Name="svc_epic_emergency*" earliest=-30d
| append [
    search index=epic sourcetype="epic:audit" AccessContext="*" earliest=-30d
    | rex field=AccessContext "(?<ctx>Downtime|Emergency)"
    | where ctx IN ("Downtime","Emergency") ]
| eval principal=coalesce(Account_Name, USER_ID)
| stats earliest(_time) as first latest(_time) as last dc(host) as systems by principal, ctx
| eval duration_h=round((last-first)/3600,2)
| sort - last
description: Audits emergency and downtime accounts touching production ePHI so procedural safeguards around emergency access are reviewable and time-bound.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for HIPAA Security compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes epic, windows and sourcetypes epic:audit, epic:audit`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Require post-event attestation form ID in separate ticketing sourcetype and join; (2) auto-expire passwords via IAM; (3) weekly privacy review of all uses. Indexes required: `index=epic`, `index=windows`.
visualization: Table (principal, duration, systems), Single value (uses per month).
knownFalsePositives: PHI access from authorized clinical workflows, billing operations, audit reviews, or BAA partners.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `WinEventLog:Security` 4624 for `svc_epic_emergency*`, `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit` `AccessContext="Downtime"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Require post-event attestation form ID in separate ticketing sourcetype and join; (2) auto-expire passwords via IAM; (3) weekly privacy review of all uses.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
(index=windows EventCode="4622" OR EventCode="4624") Account_Name="svc_epic_emergency*" earliest=-30d
| append [
    search index=epic sourcetype="epic:audit" AccessContext="*" earliest=-30d
    | rex field=AccessContext "(?<ctx>Downtime|Emergency)"
    | where ctx IN ("Downtime","Emergency") ]
| eval principal=coalesce(Account_Name, USER_ID)
| stats earliest(_time) as first latest(_time) as last dc(host) as systems by principal, ctx
| eval duration_h=round((last-first)/3600,2)
| sort - last
```

Understanding this SPL

**Emergency Access Procedure — Downtime / Break-Glass Account Usage (§164.312(a)(2)(ii))** — Audits emergency and downtime accounts touching production ePHI so procedural safeguards around emergency access are reviewable and time-bound.

Documented **Data sources**: `WinEventLog:Security` 4624 for `svc_epic_emergency*`, `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit` `AccessContext="Downtime"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: windows.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=windows, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Appends rows from a subsearch with `append`.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **principal** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by principal, ctx** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **duration_h** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t dc(Authentication.dest) as agg_value from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - agg_value
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Emergency Access Procedure — Downtime / Break-Glass Account Usage (§164.312(a)(2)(ii))** — Audits emergency and downtime accounts touching production ePHI so procedural safeguards around emergency access are reviewable and time-bound.

Documented **Data sources**: `WinEventLog:Security` 4624 for `svc_epic_emergency*`, `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit` `AccessContext="Downtime"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the EHR audit trail (Epic, Cerner), walk known-good cases from the same workflow class, and confirm index coverage, field extractions, and access permissions match expectations.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (principal, duration, systems), Single value (uses per month).
grandmaExplanation: We audits emergency and downtime accounts touching production ePHI so procedural safeguards around emergency access are reviewable and time-bound. HIPAA is about keeping health records private and secure when we use them in care and operations.
